module.title: The Gauntlet
module.author: Lantern & Blade Project
module.description: A Warden training hall under the Hollowmarch — a central foyer with eight sigiled doors, each a purpose-built chamber that exercises one mechanical surface of the Lantern & Blade rules engine. Combat flow, multi-group encounters, boss tuning, detect-then-avoid hazards, puzzle resolution with effect chaining, item pickup-and-use, consumables (all three on_use keywords), and save-or-suffer riders each get their own door. Designed for rapid regression testing during rules-engine development, not as a narrative playthrough. Walkable in ten minutes; useful after every refactor.

hall_of_initiation.name: The Hall of Initiation
hall_of_initiation.description: A circular stone chamber, domed and windowless, its floor flagged in pale limestone laid in the Warden laurel-pattern. Eight iron-banded doors are set at regular intervals around the wall, each carved above the lintel with a single sigil — arms, line, gate, foot, word, blade, herb, breath — and each lit from within its frame by the steady unshielded flame of old Warden lantern-work. A bronze plaque is set into the floor at the room's center: WALK EACH DOOR ONCE. RETURN. THE LANTERNBLADE WILL KNOW. The air is still, cold, and older than any current master of the Watch.

features.name: The Bronze Plaque
features.description: A rectangular plate of old bronze set flush into the floor at the room's center. Three lines, cleanly cast: WALK EACH DOOR ONCE. RETURN. THE LANTERNBLADE WILL KNOW.
features.on_examine: The plaque is older than the watch-houses. The phrasing is Warden initiatory: a recruit walked the eight doors in whatever order, returned to the hub between each, and the Lanternblade — or whatever ritual blade the initiate was to inherit — was held to witness the work. The 'knowing' is a lantern-craft — the blade warms visibly when its bearer has walked the full circuit. Nothing compels the order. The doors do not lock behind the initiate.
features.gm_notes: Framing beat for the test hub. The player may walk the doors in any order; the module does not enforce a sequence. If you want a completion signal, narrate the Lanternblade (or the Oathblade, if picked up) warming steadily when the player returns to the hub after the last chamber.

chamber_first_arms.name: The Chamber of First Arms
chamber_first_arms.description: A small square training cell, its walls pitted with the marks of old blade-practice. A straw training-dummy stands on a cross of wooden legs at the center of the floor — but the dummy is not cloth and wood anymore. Something old and small and thin has climbed into it, wearing the dummy the way a crow wears a coat. It lifts a practice shortsword as you cross the threshold.

back_to_hub.label: back to the hall of initiation

encounters.name: The Training Dummy
encounters.placement: The animated dummy takes a single step forward from the center of the floor, levels the practice shortsword, and comes on at a walking pace.
encounters.behavior: Mindless. Attacks until broken. Does not flee; does not parley; does not check morale.
encounters.gm_notes: Single-enemy baseline. The intended test path: player declares attack → rolls d20 + mod → hit/miss announced → damage roll → HP tracked → enemy turn. One full combat cycle against a weak enemy. If this chamber does not resolve cleanly, the combat pipeline is broken.

chamber_the_line.name: The Chamber of the Line
chamber_the_line.description: A long rectangular drill-hall. Three training-dummies stand in a line along the far wall, arranged at the spacing an old sergeant would set for a three-man shield-line. Whatever occupies the first of them takes a step down off its peg as you enter, and the other two follow a beat later, uneven — the third of them staggers like it remembers a bad knee.

back_to_hub.label: back to the hall of initiation

encounters.name: The Shield-Line
encounters.placement: Three animated dummies step down off their pegs in sequence — left, center, right — and close in a loose shield-line at fifteen feet, ten feet, five feet.
encounters.behavior: Mindless — the three fight independently rather than as a coordinated line. Standard undead behavior: attack nearest living thing, do not flee, do not check morale. The 'uneven third' narration is flavor only; all three share the practice_skeleton stat block.
encounters.gm_notes: Multi-enemy baseline. Tests the rules engine's handling of several instances in one encounter: HP tracked independently per instance, turn order resolved, enemies defeated one at a time while others continue. If the engine collapses HP or fires a shared defeat condition early, this chamber will surface it. Morale is explicitly not triggered — these are mindless undead — so if the engine rolls morale on this encounter, that's a bug. A morale-check test belongs on a different chamber (Chamber of the Line can absorb one in a future iteration by swapping in a humanoid group).

chamber_black_gate.name: The Chamber of the Black Gate
chamber_black_gate.description: A taller chamber than the others, ceiling lost in shadow. A single iron-banded door — black, scored by generations of blade-points — is set into the far wall. Standing before it, not moving, is a figure in the grey-blue coat of an old Warden officer, helmed, a bearded axe held across the body in the low guard. The officer does not speak. The eyes are the colorless blue of ice in a trough. This is what the sigil above the door means: the gate is the officer, and the officer is the gate.

back_to_hub.label: back to the hall of initiation

encounters.name: The Black Gate Warden
encounters.placement: The officer stays in the low guard until the player steps fully into the chamber, then advances at a measured walking pace, axe held two-handed. The officer closes to melee range on the first round without rolling movement — this is a set-piece; the fight begins at arm's length.
encounters.behavior: Old Warden tactics, still sharp. Opens with a two-handed axe swing. Uses Measured Cleave (see stat block) when the player is below half HP. Does not flee; does not parley; does not check morale. Collapses on defeat.
encounters.gm_notes: Boss-tier baseline. Tests heavier HP (defeat takes several rounds, not one or two), a harder to-hit bonus, and an attack with a proper crit threat against a level-3 Fighter. If the engine's damage scales misbehave against HP ~30, this chamber will show it. If crit handling is broken, the officer will be the place it shows — narrate crit natural-20s clearly and watch how the engine reports damage.

chamber_careful_foot.name: The Chamber of the Careful Foot
chamber_careful_foot.description: A long narrow chamber, the floor patterned in a regular grid of square flagstones. A number of them sit a finger's-width proud of their neighbors, and the walls on both sides are pierced at shin height with narrow slots, all of them blackened inside with old grime. The far end of the room holds nothing but the sigil-lit lintel of the return door. Whatever this chamber once taught a recruit, it taught by hurting them slightly.

back_to_hub.label: back to the hall of initiation

hazards.name: The Training Dart-Plates
hazards.description: A third of the floor's flagstones are pressure plates wired to a spring-loaded rack of training darts behind the wall. The darts are not poisoned (this was a training hall), but they are sharp enough to teach the lesson. The safe path is along the unraised flags near the walls.
hazards.gm_notes: The full detect-then-avoid hazard pipeline in one room. Tests: detection check runs first and, on success, awards the detection XP and skips the avoidance roll entirely; on detection failure, the avoidance check is offered; on avoidance failure, damage is applied AND the wounded condition is set; a successful avoidance awards its own (smaller) XP. If the engine skips straight to avoidance without offering detection, or applies damage on a detection-success, the pipeline is broken.

detection.on_success: You stop at the threshold and read the floor. Half of the raised flags sit a shade paler than their neighbors — dust-free on top, because the rack has fired enough times to keep the rising plates polished. The safe path is along the left wall for the first four flags, then a single diagonal step at the fifth seam, then the right wall the rest of the way.

avoidance.on_success: You pick your way across the hall in a string of careful half-steps, shifting your weight along the heel-line of each flag, and none of them drops.

on_failure.narration: Your third step rings hollow under the heel and the flag drops a finger-width. The rack behind the wall fires. A pair of training darts glance off your mail and one catches you in the shin, deep enough to bleed and slow you the rest of the way across.

reward_on_detection.narration: You read the floor before it reads you. The rack never fires.

chamber_hidden_word.name: The Chamber of the Hidden Word
chamber_hidden_word.description: A hexagonal chamber, smaller than the others, the ceiling lower. Set into each of five of the six walls is a plain iron door-plate — closed, unmarked — and carved above each door-plate, in Warden column-script, a single word. DUTY · TRUST · SILENCE · HONOR · TRUTH. The sixth wall holds only the return door and, carved into it in smaller letters, a single line: OF THESE FIVE, ONE IS KEPT IN THE DARK.

back_to_hub.label: back to the hall of initiation

features.name: The Hidden Word
features.description: Five words above five plates. Each door-plate is hinged to depress the width of a hand when pressed. The inscription on the return-door wall is the riddle: which of the five virtues is 'kept in the dark.'

solution.description: SILENCE. A Warden 'kept in the dark' is a Warden sworn to silence about the order's secrets. Accept variants — 'silence', 'the silent one', 'quiet', 'the keeping of secrets' — as a pure-narrative solve. If the player is stuck, the INT check is a check-gated fallback and the GM may narrate the door-plates warming faintly under the correct one.

on_success.narration: You press the SILENCE plate. The plate sinks a full handspan under your palm, and somewhere behind the walls of the hall a counterweight drops with a single clean stone-on-stone knock. In the Chamber of the Oathblade, across the foyer, a small cabinet-lock will have opened; you know this the way you know a draft has shifted in a house.

on_failure.narration: The plate sinks a handspan and rises again, unchanged. Somewhere behind the walls, nothing drops. The chamber is ready for a second attempt.

chamber_oathblade.name: The Chamber of the Oathblade
chamber_oathblade.description: A small armory-cell, barely larger than a pantry. A single iron-banded rack stands against the far wall, sized for one longsword. Above the rack, a carved laurel and a small bronze plate reading FIRST BLADE OF THE INITIATE. A practice dummy, inert this time — plain cloth and straw — stands in the corner, ready to take a swing from whoever has drawn from the rack.

back_to_hub.label: back to the hall of initiation

features.name: The Initiate's Rack
features.description: A heavy oak-and-iron sword-rack against the far wall, sized for a single longsword. The cabinet beneath the rack is closed with a plain iron latch, lantern-sealed. Until the Chamber of the Hidden Word is walked, the latch will not lift.
features.on_success: The latch lifts without resistance, the cabinet opens, and you find what the initiates were given: a plain silvered longsword of clean Warden make, wrapped in oilcloth, one laurel stamped on the crossguard. This is an Oathblade. It is meant to be carried.
features.on_failure: The latch is shut and the cabinet resists. On a second pass it opens easily — the latch was never the obstacle.
features.prereq_hint: The cabinet beneath the rack is lantern-sealed. Walking the Chamber of the Hidden Word first will unseal it.

encounters.name: The Practice Dummy

trigger.condition: The player draws the Oathblade from the rack for the first time. The dummy in the corner stirs — the Wardens wired one into every armory-cell so the recruit could feel the new blade land before they walked out.

encounters.placement: The practice dummy in the corner unfolds upright, lifts a training staff, and steps into the middle of the floor. It is weaker than the dummies in First Arms; it is a target, not an opponent.
encounters.behavior: Mindless. Swings the staff. Does not pursue; does not flee.
encounters.gm_notes: Tests the equip-and-use flow: player picks up the Oathblade, equips it to main_hand (likely displacing whatever they were carrying), then attacks the dummy. If the engine does not apply the Oathblade's attack_bonus +1 and bonus_damage 1d4 radiant to the swing, the weapon-swap pipeline is broken. Single encounter; should resolve in one or two rounds. Treats as from_bestiary for XP.

chamber_apothecary.name: The Chamber of the Apothecary
chamber_apothecary.description: A long narrow chamber lined on both walls with wooden shelving. The shelves hold a great many glass vials, small clay jars, and folded oilcloth bundles; most of them are empty or cracked, but three bundles near eye height on the right-hand wall are plainly intact, oilcloth still tied, each with a small tag in a neat Warden hand.

back_to_hub.label: back to the hall of initiation

features.name: The Shelf — Healing Draughts
features.description: The left-most of the three intact bundles. The tag reads, in Warden column-hand: FOR THE WOUNDED.
features.on_success: The bundle is heavier than it looks. Inside: two red-brown vials in a leather cradle, stoppered in wax. The wax is fresh.
features.gm_notes: Tests the `heal_player` on_use path. The player should pick these up, then, ideally, drink one when wounded (e.g. after Careful Foot or the Black Gate Warden) to verify the HP-restoration pipeline fires and clamps correctly at max HP.

features.name: The Shelf — Antitoxins
features.description: The middle bundle. The tag reads: FOR THE POISONED.
features.on_success: A pair of narrow clay vials, both stoppered with wax and wrapped in felt. The felt is cold; the clay is colder.
features.gm_notes: Tests the `cure_condition` on_use path. The intended dev test: walk Breath-Held first to acquire the poisoned condition, then come here, then use antitoxin to verify the condition is correctly removed.

features.name: The Shelf — Hallowed Water
features.description: The right-most bundle. The tag reads: FOR THE UNQUIET.
features.on_success: A single small glass vial, stoppered with a fitted wooden plug. The water inside is still and clear and, when held against the lantern, casts a faint pale light onto the wall behind it.
features.gm_notes: Tests the `gm_adjudicate` on_use path — holy_water's on_use is pure prose. The engine should surface a 'use item?' confirmation, and on confirm the GM narrates the effect from the consumable prose. No mechanical resolution required. If the engine tries to resolve a heal or a condition-cure here, the dispatch on_use keyword is broken.

chamber_breath_held.name: The Chamber of the Breath-Held
chamber_breath_held.description: A small square cell, ceiling low. A thin pale mist pools on the flagstones, knee-deep, still as standing water. The mist has a faint acrid smell — not painful, but noticeable, and the eyes water on the second breath. The far door is clearly visible through the fog; it is four strides off.

back_to_hub.label: back to the hall of initiation

hazards.name: The Acrid Mist
hazards.description: A pale mist pools on the floor, chemically treated — the Wardens used it as a breath-discipline trial. A held breath or a damp cloth at the mouth is the old initiate's answer; a lungful without preparation costs the recruit a day's unsteady stomach and, sometimes, the chamber.
hazards.gm_notes: Pure-avoidance hazard — no detection block. Tests the save pipeline: trigger on entry, CON save, on failure damage AND condition applied. The intended dev test: walk in deliberately without preparation, fail the CON save, watch the engine apply 1d4 damage and set the 'poisoned' condition. Then walk to Apothecary, use antitoxin, verify the condition clears. If the engine applies damage but not the condition (or vice versa), the hazard dispatch is broken. If the engine offers a detection roll here, the detection-vs-avoidance branch logic is broken.

avoidance.on_success: You take a careful breath at the threshold, hold it, and cross the cell in three long strides. The eyes water, the mouth tastes of old copper, but the chest stays still.

on_failure.narration: You take one unguarded breath at the threshold and the next three strides get harder than they should. By the time your hand touches the far door your stomach is tight, your vision is running at the edges, and the cell has taught its lesson.

module_bestiary.name: The Gauntlet — Module Foes
module_bestiary.description: Two bespoke stat blocks for the Gauntlet test hub. practice_skeleton is a deliberately weak training construct used in First Arms, The Line, and the Oathblade chamber; black_gate_warden is a boss-tier officer for the Black Gate chamber. Both are unhallowed Warden lantern-constructs, not true undead — they move on old lantern-work wired into the chamber, not on their own will.

practice_skeleton.name: Animated Training Dummy
practice_skeleton.description: A cloth-and-straw training dummy wired into the chamber's lantern-work. When a recruit crosses the threshold, the dummy lifts its practice weapon and takes a step down off its peg. It is not a true undead — no bone, no memory, no hunger — only old Warden craft doing the work of a sparring partner.

special_abilities.name: Mindless
special_abilities.description: Cannot be charmed, frightened, or reasoned with. Does not make saves against effects that target the mind.

practice_skeleton.behavior: Mindless. Attacks until broken. Does not flee.
practice_skeleton.tactics: Straight melee with the training weapon. No tricks. A recruit's first-combat baseline.

black_gate_warden.name: The Black Gate Warden
black_gate_warden.description: A lantern-construct in the coat and helm of an old Warden officer, animated by the chamber's oldest and heaviest piece of work. It does not speak; it does not pursue beyond the chamber. Within the chamber, it is the gate.

attacks.name: Measured Cleave
attacks.on_hit: Used when the target is below half HP. A clean two-handed downward cut — heavier than the standard swing, slower to wind up, unmistakable on the narration.

special_abilities.name: Mindless
special_abilities.description: Cannot be charmed, frightened, or reasoned with. Does not make saves against effects that target the mind.

special_abilities.name: Bound to Chamber
special_abilities.description: Will not pursue beyond the threshold of the Black Gate chamber. If the player retreats to the hub, the construct returns to its guard-post.

black_gate_warden.behavior: Old Warden tactics, still sharp. Opens in low guard; advances at a measured walking pace; uses Measured Cleave when the player is below half HP. Does not flee; does not parley.
black_gate_warden.tactics: Boss-tier regression target. Tests heavier HP and a clean crit threat (1d10+3 on the normal swing, 2d6+3 on the cleave). If the Oathblade is equipped, the +1 radiant and the silvered rider should both engage against the 'unhallowed' tag on this construct.
